title: narcissus calcicola subsp. onubensis
intro: Narcissus calcicola subsp. onubensis is a subspecies of the narcissus calcicola plant, a type of flowering bulbous perennial found in southern Spain. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Narcissus calcicola subsp. onubensis involve using the bulb and flowers in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its potential medicinal properties.
